headline: GOLD SLIDES TO $4,613 AS INFLATION FEARS WEIGH; BULL RUN SHOWING FATIGUE
snippet: Gold edged lower to $4,613/oz on May 3 as inflation concerns weighed on risk appetite, snapping a recent run near record highs. The retreat follows a $1,351 gain over the past year; analysts are watching the $4,500 level as near-term support while sovereign and central bank demand continues to underpin the long-term bid.

headline: CHINA VACUUMS UP RECORD 836 TONNES OF SILVER IN MARCH — SOLAR BOOM WIDENS GLOBAL DEFICIT
snippet: China imported an all-time record 836 tonnes of silver in March 2026 — well above seasonal averages — driven by surging solar panel manufacturing and a surge in retail investment demand, according to GoldSeek's Gold SWOT analysis. The world's largest silver consumer has now taken in more than 790 tonnes in aggregate through early 2026, deepening the structural global supply gap that now spans six consecutive years.

headline: COT REPORT MAY 1: GOLD AND SILVER SPEC LONGS HOLD FIRM; DOLLAR FAILS TO SUSTAIN REBOUNDS
snippet: The May 1 Commitments of Traders report shows speculative net-long positioning in gold and silver holding broadly stable despite the recent price pullback, while the USD index continues to fail at resistance levels. Analysts interpret the stable longs as evidence that the metals bull market remains intact and that traders are waiting for a fresh catalyst rather than unwinding positions.

headline: CHINA'S SULFURIC ACID BAN TAKES EFFECT IN MAY — CHILE'S COPPER OUTPUT FACES SUPPLY CRUNCH
snippet: China's halt of sulfuric acid exports — triggered by Middle East conflict cutting sulphur shipments from Iran — is now live in May, directly threatening roughly 20% of Chilean copper output that relies on acid-leach processing. Chile imports over one million tonnes of Chinese sulfuric acid annually; spot acid prices at Mejillones port have already surged 58% since February, squeezing margins for SX-EW copper producers.

headline: GOLD STOCKS TRADING AT ONLY 9.6X CASH FLOW — HAYWOOD: EQUITIES HAVEN'T CAUGHT UP TO $4,600 GOLD
snippet: Haywood Securities argues senior gold producers remain deeply undervalued at 9.6x forward cash flow — barely above the five-year average of 8.35x — even as gold trades near $4,600/oz. The firm forecasts gold averaging $4,906/oz in 2026, calls recent pullbacks "an attractive entry point," and flags record producer margins as the catalyst for eventual equity re-rating that has eluded the sector throughout the bull market.

headline: CORE LITHIUM'S FINNISS MINE RESTARTS ORE EXTRACTION IN MAY AFTER TWO-YEAR SHUTDOWN
snippet: Core Lithium's Finniss lithium mine in the Northern Territory is resuming ore extraction this month after two years in care-and-maintenance, backed by A$290 million in financing that includes a Glencore convertible note and a Nebari senior debt facility. First spodumene concentrate shipments are targeted for Q4 2026 — a timely restart as Morgan Stanley forecasts an 80,000-tonne LCE deficit in 2026, reversing last year's surplus.

headline: NEXGEN ENERGY TO BREAK GROUND THIS SUMMER ON ROOK I — CANADA'S LARGEST URANIUM PROJECT
snippet: NexGen Energy will begin construction this summer on its Rook I uranium mine in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin after receiving the first new Canadian uranium mine construction licence since 2004 from the CNSC. The Arrow deposit at Rook I could produce nearly 30 million lb of U3O8 annually in its first five years from one of the world's highest-grade undeveloped uranium resources, generating an estimated $32.5 billion in economic impact for Saskatchewan.

headline: BIG FUNDS POUR $8.24B INTO MINING IN Q1 2026 — INSTITUTIONAL MONEY BETS ON SUPERCYCLE
snippet: Major institutional funds poured $8.24 billion into the mining sector in Q1 2026 as managers bet on a sustained commodity supercycle, with capital flowing simultaneously into gold, silver, copper, and critical minerals. Analysts cite the convergence of supply constraints, energy-transition demand, and sovereign diversification away from U.S. Treasuries as compressing the timeline for peak commodity deficits.

headline: ARIZONA METALS RELEASES KAY MINE PEA: $445M NPV AT SPOT PRICES ON 9.28MT AT 3.18% CuEq
snippet: Arizona Metals Corp. (TSX: AMC) released a PEA on April 30 for its Kay Mine VMS project in Arizona, reporting an after-tax NPV5% of US$445 million and an IRR of 14.9% under spot price assumptions ($6.05/lb Cu, $4,745/oz Au, $77.48/oz Ag). The indicated resource stands at 9.28 million tonnes grading 3.18% CuEq, with the project's economics improving sharply as base metal and gold prices trade near multi-year highs.

headline: RARE EARTHS DEAL WEEK: CHINA TIGHTENS QUIETLY WHILE WESTERN CONSOLIDATION ACCELERATES
snippet: The week of April 27 – May 1 saw simultaneous moves on both sides of the rare earth supply chain: China issued new enforcement rules tightening control over rare earth mining and separation, while Western producers accelerated consolidation with Critical Metals lifting its Tanbreez stake to 92.5% and Rare Earths Americas targeting a $368 million U.S. IPO. Analysts note that China's 45% Q2 price hike for rare earth concentrate is now filtering into downstream magnet and EV supply chains.
